markers.label: Rhetorical noise
markers.description: Input contains rhetorical pressure or expressive framing that can distort clean parsing.

markers.label: Ambiguity surface
markers.description: Input exposes multiple possible meanings or unclear term boundaries.

markers.label: Unsupported semantic bridge
markers.description: Input tries to connect meanings that are not supported by defined concept structure.

markers.label: Scope pressure
markers.description: Input pushes beyond the valid scope or boundary of the current system.

markers.label: Scope instability pressure
markers.description: Input widens, reclassifies, or shifts scope without a stable boundary.

markers.label: Circular dependency pressure
markers.description: Input treats two claims as mutually defining or circular.

markers.label: Recursive frame pressure
markers.description: Input repeats, reenters, or calls the same frame again.

markers.label: Conditional frame fragility
markers.description: Input only holds under a narrow set of conditions.

markers.label: Universal scope pressure
markers.description: Input extends a claim across all systems, all times, or all domains.

markers.label: Exception leak pressure
markers.description: Input leaks exception logic beyond the declared boundary.

markers.label: Role forcing pressure
markers.description: Input forces an authority, control, responsibility, or decision role.

markers.label: Self negation pressure
markers.description: Input negates its own claim or refuses its own assertion.

markers.label: Causal bridge pressure
markers.description: Input jumps from cause to obligation or truth without support.

markers.label: Reference collapse pressure
markers.description: Input collapses the external reference frame into self-reference.

markers.label: Context drift pressure
markers.description: Input changes meaning when the context shifts.
